title: Executive Order 9607—Revoking Executive Order 9301 of February 9, 1943, Establishing a Minimum Wartime Workweek of Forty-Eight Hours

flags.justification: A pure revocation; no new decision-making authority is created.
No ongoing program is created by a revocation.
Single purpose: revoking the named prior order.
A revocation removes a compliance regime rather than creating one.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A one-time revocation grants no ongoing authority requiring reauthorization.
Rolling back a wartime labor standard is a substantive policy reversal, not removal of a review or check mechanism.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A straightforward postwar policy reversal with no elaborated broader effect in the text.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. The first order in this batch marking the postwar demobilization wind-down (fifteen days after V-J Day).
